metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_939
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The authors use an antibody against the extracellular domain Lasso, a cleaved fragment of teneurin-2 that stays membrane-anchored, for immunoEM of synaptosomes. Fig.4 F,G,I shows the postsynaptic localization.
This postsynaptic localization is supported by a biochemical sub-fractionation of synaptosomes shown in Fig.4H.
Fig.3 shows the interaction of Lasso/LPH1 in NBa2 cells which supports a membrane localization of Lasso.

22/6/2017 Pim
- "extrinsic component" --> Teneurin is cleaved after the TMR on the extracellular side. It is therefore not an integral protein. Yet, it stays membrane anchored as show in Figs. 3-5; this is probably through binding to cell surface proteins.
- Accession Q9R1K2-2 was retrieved by BLAST of human lasso protein sequence against rat uniprot proteins. The best scoring reviewed sequence was selected with match score:
E-value: 0.0
Score: 13616
Ident.: 97.8%
Positives : 99.1%
Query Length: 2637
Match Length: 2765
One unreviewed sequence (A0A096P6L6) had marginally better score but this seems to endode an equivalent lasso sequence.. Experimental description: rat brain synaptosomes were used for immunoEM and fractionation.

Fig.3 used human Lasso: "Full-length Lasso cDNA (GenBank accession no. JF784339) was reconstructed by conventional cloning from ESTs and fragments obtained by PCR amplification of human
brain cDNA (AMS Biotechnology)." Species is disregarded for annotation.
